title: thymus praecox subsp. trachselianus
intro: Thymus praecox subsp. trachselianus, also known as Alpine Thyme or Wild Thyme, is a subspecies of the thyme plant, characterized by its small, fragrant flowers and delicate, pinkish-purple color. Traditionally, the leaves and flowers of Alpine Thyme have been used in herbal medicine for their antimicrobial and antiseptic properties, which help to combat infections and promote wound healing. The health benefits of Alpine Thyme include its ability to alleviate respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, as well as its potential to reduce stress and improve digestion. Alpine Thyme can be prepared in various ways, including teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
